content:
To be clear, the point of the discussion is about the fact that the way it's written currently is *actively contradictory* to the actual way the mechanic works, and the fact that *I had absolutely no idea* because the text is so misleading.

If you can imagine the game's mechanics and functions (code) as an independent language, then you in particular can understand the difficulty of trying to proofread—let alone translate—English text when you don't understand or have access to the source language, whether that be Chinese or Mechanics.

We need a solid understanding of the mechanics to be able to write this correctly. Every piece of information provided by people like Jynn is invaluable for a proper translation. Lens cited Jynn's guide last night and it really helped. Thanks Jynn!
yeah I agree that it should be changed
not a fan of the proposed one currently
which is why I'm not going full steam ahead with it.
I'm gonna move over to Mythag University discord and chat about it with more people to get more opinions before making a decision
but I would support whatever changes provided I can get some confirmation that the devs both want it to happen and are willing to ensure that they can add things necessary to make it work

I've been told that the priority is on text that is broken, wrong, or misleading.
This isn't broken, but it is wrong and misleading.
I would argue this is well worth the work.
i do think the proposed options should be floated by a larger base of players to gauge general opinion
though i'd really much prefer an official avenue in the official discord to do it with...mythag university being an unofficial fan discord makes it feel less trustworthy to base opinions off of
...but we don't have that, so
given the lack of other options it'd be nice to have a poll with a decent amount of votes in it, probably use the mythag uni forum and have them ping everyone?

Yeah, MU is the biggest space I can get that isn't full of 🫃 
For some reason I feel like the official discord is a bit too unserious to get good feedback from.
the official discord is a complete mess
because it's unmoderated
there are good people there but they're drowned out by the swarms of people who treat it as an unmoderated dumping ground

They've given me a dedicated forum there, there's an opt-in role in the server for people who want to participate in this kind of discussion, and gave me permissions to @ that role and @ everyone in the server if needed. I've gotten some pretty good feedback out of there so far, I trust them to know what they're talking about
-# *(mostly...)*
I was just collecting screenshots of our chat last night to use as a start for the discussion, do you want me to anonymize you? <@88644018673582080>
my main concern though is that those people (presumably) know the game decently enough, and my concern is moreso about new players
that is definitely a concern that will be considered.
I'm not really a fan of Amplify either, but I can't think of anything better right now.

I did ask some HSR people in a different server and they found it confusing
but well HSR is kinda...
nah no worries
I should probably join and get a role too and participate more actively in there
because the official discord is.....not really it
it's gotten worse over time but recently it's just been way too much
feel free to @ me when you post it if you want
can I get an invite to the server again
https://discord.gg/XJxmccnq
what happened?
it's completely unmoderated so it's just a mess of chaotic nonsense completely unrelated to the game
pretty much entirely just spam and memes
it's not really possible to have a proper discussion about the game or a detailed conversation in there anymore (the english channel, I mean)
oofs... i took a peek and .. yea...
chaos

Woah wait there's someone new here
<@1384428399061106739> are you the new localization hire??

Okay so after sleeping on the Aliemus verbs thing, I've come to the conclusion that the only acceptable way forward is with the plain-English "**Increase**", as it's too risky to put potentially confusing language on such a staple effect.

1. `Deal 200 DMG. Increase Aliemus by 10.`
2. `Increase the Aliemus of the Awakener with the least Aliemus by 30.`
3. `Increase Aliemus by 20 for Thais and another target Awakener.`

But that then shifts the problem off to, ironically, the same phrasing that would likely need to be the solution to the STR Bonus Problem™. I really dislike the mangled language required to describe Aliemus% if we use "Increase" as the Aliemus verb, but I think it would be best to have that one (relatively rare) phrasing be the unfortunate casualty required to make the rest of the game's text more readable.

Instead of going to the community to discuss what the Aliemus verb should be (and saying "we can't use increase because that's for bonuses"), I'm going to go there to discuss whether or not we should use "Increase" for bonuses. It's very possible to use it for both so long as we can accept the horrendous result of their intersection.
I will never be against simple wording with clear meanings (for gameplay)
well what would be an example of a horrendous intersection
I agree with this 100%
well that's the devs problem not ours
TBF though it's not like gacha games don't get away with worthless tutorials
hi Limbus

anyways I think gain is a very easy to understand "keyword", since even if they don't know what Aliemus is a new player will be on the lookout for something to have increased, so they'll eventually look up, see the yellow bar increased, and immediately connect "aliemus = this yellow bar" while amplify sounds more like a buff
so they might be more inclined to looking at their buff bar
I dunno that's my immediate thought anyway
but if amplify was underlined an dyou could hold click on it and it says "increases your aliemus"
anyways I think "increase" is also fine
it'll just make the sentences a little wordier

Though, some conversations with Ace have definitely given me another very important factor to consider, and that's the way they have the MTL set up.

It's clear currently that—whether this distinction exists in Chinese or not—the MTL translates the Aliemus verb into a passive voice very consistently, and I'm unsure how difficult that would be to change. I think the only way to do this is to identify all of the unique phrases for this in both English and Chinese, and provide phrase translations instead of word translations. God forbid we end up in `Amplify 10 Aliemus` territory.

but for things that increase the amount of aliemus gained you'd have to either A) make Amplify a keyword with all that that entails or B) just say "Aliemus gained increases by %" and I think it's a lot easier to do B for the devs
I'm not opposed to either option to be clear
but the lack of direct dev communication means I have to assume the devs will take the easiest route possible
so while it'd be nice to be able to clean everything up and add fancy keywords and make the game as unique as it could be
I don't think the devs will want to do that much work for it

That's the thing, is this whole thing is predicated on how confusing Aliemus% is, and the fact that NOTHING had prepared me for the fact that Aliemus% is giver-side, not receiver-side.

We need an active-voice verb, not a passive-voice one. "Aliemus gained increases by %" immediately sets us back to square 0 where I thought that effect would increase the Aliemus given by Embryos.
yeah then I gues it could be like
"Aliemus gained by own Command Cards increased by X%" or some shit like that
which reads stupid
but it is technically correct

"own" is incorrect there
"Command" and "Cards" are also incorrect there
https://giphy.com/gifs/idk-umamusume-agnes-tachyon-PXvLqmaLAGaYXJKfIr
what's wrong with what I said exactly? in this example the Aliemus gain is irrelevant and no Doresain card with Aliemus gain was used so the stat doesn't apply
in this case if it normally granted 10 Aliemus then it'd give 15 right
and here it's his own strike Command Card

Right, but consider the case of team-wide Aliemus% like Horla's Joy. You can't use the posessive "own" there because the posessor is null. And I was saying Command/Card is incorrect because Exalts also have Aliemus% applied
isn't that an entirely different stat though
Aliemus Regen
Thais Exalt

and the confusion of active/passive is that I assumed the *Awakener that has higher Aliemus%* would *receive more Aliemus*, when actually it's the other way around. If I use Thais Exalt and she has 0 A%, and my other Awakeners have 10%, 20% and 30% respectively, then if Thais says she grants 10 Aliemus, everyone else would receive *exactly 10 Aliemus.*
yeah that tracks

But because of the passive verbs we've been using, I had assumed they would gain 11, 12, and 13 respectively, AND I had assumed *before that* that things like An Innocent Thanks and Embryo would also give increasing amounts of Aliemus based on the target's A%. Which is false
I was *also* informed last night that Aliemus Regen is not Aliemus%. So yeah, that sucks too but that's a different problem for a different day
yeah I think I'm getting confused with all this extra explanation lol
I tend to do that. Sorry...
so basically Aliemus% is meant for yourself
is what this all boils down to yes

A% is an outward stat.
The *source* of the Aliemus-increasing effect needs A%, NOT the target.
so what would be a 1-2 sentence way to write this type of effect
If my Thais has 50% A%, and the rest of my party has 0%, then if Thais would usually grant 10 Aliemus, she grants everyone 15 instead.
`Aliemus amplified by Awakeners' effects increases by X%.`
ok and what if we can't keyword amplify
Then the English translation of the game suffers accordingly.
Doesn't have to be amplify *specifically,* just has to be something that works that's not *increase.*
unless we want to replace Increases/Increased in that sentence with something else, which is also an option.
`Awakeners' effects that increase Aliemus are buffed by X%`
I had to edit that one to make sense but it still doesn't, hold on
to be clear this effect is specifically the aliemus the user gives to others/to themselves?
`Awakeners' Aliemus-increasing effects are buffed by X%`

specifically the Aliemus the *Awakener* gives to others/themselves.
but it would be best if we can use the same verb for *all* Aliemus-increasing effects, like Buff cards, Relics or Embryo.
that's why I proposed the sentence structure above, is that it works for both scenarios and makes it clear that the source is important.
is there an issue with using a more complex sentence like Aliemus gained and granted (not saying we use these specific terms, just using an easy to think of example)
Yes, because if Lens is correct about how Aliemus works, we need to avoid passive-voice verbs like Gain like the plague.
also now this makes me wonder if Horla's aliemus stance is her giving everybody aliemus% or if she's projecting aliemus%
there are no passive-voice Aliemus effects.
sorry to clarify what's the issue with passive voice
Aliemus% is a personal/subjective stat, so team-wide Aliemus% is the same as team-wide Crit%, that being that each Awakener's A% is increased by the same amount.

"Aliemus gained by the wielder is increased by X%".
Okay, then why the FUCK does using an Embryo on them only give them the same amount no matter how much that increase is?
I think that's moreso a problem with how the description/sentence is written rather than it being passive
unless I'm misunderstanding something
that's what active/passive voice is?

but some words are inherently active/passive as well. Using gain here sucks:
`Gain Aliemus by 10.`
`Gain another target Awakener 10 Aliemus.`
and using increase here sucks:
`Increase 10 Aliemus.`
`Thais increases 10 Aliemus.`
"The Aliemus provided by the Awakener's Exalt/Command Cards is increased by X%" would remove Embryo/contextual stage cards, or replace Exalt/Command Cards with whatever list of effects it applies to
not married to provided just trying to use more words than granted

Correct, this is effectively the same as what I was proposing. Provide is a mostly active verb, and is being used in active voice in this sentence. but replace "provided" with "gained" and you get a VERY mechanically misleading sentence.
only reason I'm looking at gain so hard is because it's the most direct 1:1 to STS
which uses gain for pretty much every effect like this
and obviously this game is more complex than STS mechanically
yet, Slay the Spire this game is not.
but it's also the closest thing we can call a predecesor by their own admission
Slay the Spire does not have multiple playable characters simultaneously.
there is no subject or object necessary to define.
all effects are either "you", or "the opponent".
basically we need a direct line to the devs to see how willing they are to push things forward

if the devs say "Sure we'll help with keywords and shit" I'd 100% support adding this kind of thing but unless it's made abundantly clear what terms are I don't want to imagine a world where new players have to run to discord to ask "what does amplify mean" and then have to go through this multiparagraph discussion on what is or isn't aliemus%

As long as it's implemented consistently and is reasonable to continue doing so, they clearly have an interest in a good translation of the game, and that's what I'm here to do.

Like I said, the difficulty *actually* is that we need to provide *all* of these phrases translated correctly all at once (which I can do), and need to provide phrase translations instead of word translations.
did Ace say the devs are interested in this
Yes.
This *specifically?* obviously no, because it was literally conceptualized about two hours ago.
so they're willing to add new keywords and other things to clarify the game
This isn't necessarily a keyword, it's a keyword action, which doesn't need the devs involved, it only needs the MTL to be configured correctly.
Which again, if I understand correctly, is solved by providing **phrase translations**.
has amplify been used in a similar context in any notable roguelikes card games or similar such that an average gamer could understand it without needing to spent time outside the game to research it
has aliemus been used in a similar context in any notable roguelikes card games or similar such that an average gamer could understand it without needing to spent time outside the game to research it
no and I already said what I thought about it earlier
however when you combine 2 unknown words it's a lot harder to infer information then when you use a common word with an uncommon word
sorry at this point I'm getting a little snarky so I'll take a breather.
I gotta cook dinner anyways
I'll talk to the wider community about this and field more opinions later, because this is particuarly important

<:aurita_beg2:1407642015428116541> a little late to the conversation but maybe the aliemus guide i made a couple of months ago would help in understanding aliemus altogether?
https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1387172781829128304
to be clear the point of the discussion isn't really about that it's about how it should be written
to be clear, any aliemus% increasing/decreasing effects will also affect the aliemus granted by the awakener's WoD as well, so that would have to be written in as well
yeah I'm not gonna pretend that sentence was how it should be
that was something I slapped out in 5 seconds
swell and surge sound terrible, too much stigma tied to bodily functions
amplify sounds cool, I like that one
I agree re:raise and increase
boost could work but it tends to imply some kind of buff status to me
I mean the issue I see is that irrespective of verbage certain terms are already popular/exist so they're very easy to understand
tbh prior to around last night i was always of the opinion "what's wrong with obtain"
but then i paged through character skills for a while to see how existing wording is being used
and now I hate it with a passion
(I still like gain though)
I think it's moreso a matter of
how much info do you want to hide behind keywords
and how willing are devs to let us add more keywords

The other consideration is how we would word the Aliemus% effects, and having a keyword action means it has to fit there too. We're using Increase already in that context to describe that a value is going up based on another value, so we can't use it twice here (Aliemus increased by Awakeners' effects increases by %? eugh)

With that I agree that Escalate, Amplify and Intensify are our best options here, at least given the default structure of those kind of sentences:

`Aliemus amplified by Awakeners increases by X%.` / `Aliemus escalated by(...)` / `Aliemus intensified by(...)`

because it's either A) go full yugioh and have minimal/no keywords and 30 paragraph long effects B) go full hearthstone and just say fuck it infer the effect after it's used or C) go full magic and expect players to either have a keyword searcher readily available or memorize all the keywords they vomit out every year
as a yugioh player I might be biased
lol
as a magic player I might be biased
I've played a lot of different card games and each style has its strengths and weaknesses
but you need to actually support it
yeah in the end that's always been my biggest concern
so if we want to add fancy keyword names
without the devs actively supporting us it's kind of a doomed project
the devs have to be ready and willing to help make it easy to say
hold click on amplify
so they can see what that means
like personally I'd love for there to be more shit to click on to expand info
like having the actual calculation for how damage is being calculated on a card by card basis
but I don't think the devs are gonna do that
I mean, I really don't think something like that needs explaining. Amplify is simple enough to get the point across.
I dunno if I saw "Amplify Aliemus" I wouldn't immediately understand what any of that shit means
sure I could connec tthe dots after using it
and if it's on literally half of every Awakener's kit then it gets pretty clear pretty fast
*tbf I think Aliemus itself is already a confusing term*
I was about to cite this, legit. I don't think we need to explain what "amplify" means in terms of Aliemus when we never needed to explain what "Aliemus" is other than "this bar go up"

I think that a player who has a passing understanding of roguelikes IE has played STS should be able to hop onto Morimens and understand within like 3-5 minutes what the average combat experience should work like
my concern is adding a ton of keywords thrown at them without any explanation is going to be very confusing
Hey man, tell that to Leap.
honestly I think this just means we need more expansive tutorials
yup, but also be wary of tutorials that are *too* expansive. God knows I've quit a couple of games because they gave me way too much information way too early.
